title: US-Israel Strike Pasteur Institute in Iran on Day 35; Iran Death Toll Reaches 2,076
detail: On Day 35 of the US-Israel war on Iran (April 3, 2026), US and Israeli forces struck the century-old Pasteur Institute — Iran's premier biomedical research center near Tehran — along with a military-logistics bridge and two steel plants supplying dual-use materials for Iran's weapons program. The strike on the Pasteur Institute drew widespread international condemnation, with scientists and public health officials protesting the targeting of civilian research infrastructure. Iran's foreign ministry accused Israel and the US of 'deliberately targeting scientific heritage.' The cumulative Iranian death toll since February 28 reached at least 2,076 killed and 26,500 wounded, with over 600 schools damaged across Iran. The Strait of Hormuz remained closed; no commercial tanker traffic was reported on April 3. The intensifying Iran war continued to divert international attention from Gaza, where Phase 2 ceasefire negotiations remained completely stalled and humanitarian conditions were deteriorating sharply.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Day 35 of US-Israeli attacks on Iran: What is happening? April 3, 2026
Reuters — US-Israel strikes on Iranian infrastructure, Day 35, April 3, 2026
Times of Israel — Iran war Day 35 liveblog, April 3, 2026

media.caption: Day 35 of US-Israeli attacks on Iran: Pasteur Institute, bridge, and steel plants struck

title: IDF Chief Zamir Suspends Reserve Battalion Over CNN Crew Assault in West Bank
detail: IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir ordered the suspension of a reserve infantry battalion after footage emerged of IDF soldiers assaulting and detaining a CNN news crew in the West Bank on March 31. The CNN crew had been filming settler activity when they were physically confronted, equipment seized, and detained for several hours before release. Zamir's order — announced in early April and reported by CNN, JTA, and Haaretz — reflected growing institutional concern about IDF conduct at a time when the military is simultaneously fighting on five fronts (Gaza, West Bank, Lebanon border, Iran theater, Houthi defense) and facing a sustainability crisis. A Euronews analysis dated April 2 reported IDF Chief Zamir had warned the armed forces could 'collapse on itself' under the strain of five concurrent fronts and escalating settler violence demands. The incident added to a tally of over 220 journalists killed covering the conflict since October 7, 2023 — the highest journalist death toll in any conflict in CPJ history.

sources.name: CNN — IDF unit suspended after assaulting CNN crew in West Bank, April 2026
JTA — IDF suspends reserve battalion that detained CNN crew in West Bank, April 2026

title: Gaza Phase 2 Ceasefire Negotiations Remain Completely Stalled; Food Prices at 305%; Half-Ration Crisis
detail: As of April 3, 2026 — nearly six months after the October 10, 2025 ceasefire — Phase 2 negotiations between Israel and Hamas remained completely stalled, with no progress on Hamas disarmament proposals or Israeli military withdrawal timelines. NPR and multiple analysts reported the Iran war has entirely frozen Trump administration attention to Gaza diplomacy. A Hamas disarmament proposal formally presented by mediators on March 24 described a gradual 8-month process, but Hamas had not publicly accepted. UNSC data showed 689 Palestinians killed in Gaza since the October 2025 ceasefire — more than the entire Israeli death toll in the October 7 attack. Humanitarian conditions were at their worst since the ceasefire: Gaza food price inflation reached 305% in March 2026 (up from 153% in February), driven by an 80% collapse in aid truck deliveries since the Iran war began February 28. OCHA reported 1.6 million people (77% of Gaza's population) facing high acute food insecurity, with 120,000 families receiving only 50% of minimum caloric needs. Indonesia suspended participation in the Board of Peace International Stabilization Force due to regional tensions, reducing the five-country peacekeeping commitment. The UN Security Council's April 2026 high-level open debate (chaired by Bahrain) was expected to address consolidating the ceasefire and halting West Bank settlement expansion.

sources.name: NPR — Trump's peace plan for Gaza stalls as Iran war grinds on, March 12, 2026
UN OCHA — Humanitarian Situation Report, March 19, 2026
Security Council Report — April 2026 Monthly Forecast, The Middle East including the Palestinian Question
Al Jazeera — How Iran war deepens Gaza crisis, March 9, 2026

title: West Bank: IDF Operation Iron Wall Continues; 32+ Palestinians Killed in West Bank in 2026
detail: IDF Operation Iron Wall — launched January 21, 2025 — continued its active phase in Jenin, Tulkarm, and Nur Shams refugee camps on April 3. Two IDF battalions remain permanently stationed in the emptied camps, constituting the largest West Bank civilian displacement since 1967, with 40,000+ refugees displaced from Jenin and Tulkarm. As of early April 2026, over 32 Palestinians had been killed in the West Bank in 2026, with 8 killed by settlers — compared to 17 in all of 2025. A confidential document reported by Haaretz showed Netanyahu's office had issued instructions to crack down on settler violence, which analysts described as an attempt to 'silence international criticism while advancing the Judaization of the West Bank.' The UNSC April 2026 monthly forecast noted Israeli settler violence incidents had reached an average of 105 Palestinian injuries per month in 2026, up from 69 in 2025. 257 incidents of extremist settler violence were recorded in the first 25 days of the Iran war alone. Settler attacks since January 1, 2026 had displaced 1,500+ Palestinians — nearly matching the entire 2025 displacement total.

sources.name: UN Security Council Report — April 2026 Monthly Forecast
Haaretz — Netanyahu order on settler violence; IDF source on political pressure, March 31–April 1, 2026
UNRWA — Operation Iron Wall situation report, March–April 2026
